---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
---

<!-- the section below is shown only if rendering is beamer from latex. (and format:revealjs is commmented) -->

::: {.content-visible when-format="beamer"}
```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright 
\emph{4.1 - Validity: Introduction}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```

## Validity and Goal
\centering
![](Figures/target.png){width="80%"}

## Classifications and definitions of Validities

There are many different definitions and classifications of Validity!

## Validity: a definition

_"Validity is the extent to which a test accurately measures what it is supposed to measure."_

\pause
\vfill
  
_“Validity is the scientific utility of a measuring instrument, broadly statable in
terms of how well it measures what it purports to measure”_ (Nunnally & Bernstein, 1987)



## Speaking about Validity

It doesn't make sense to say that **a test is valid**
\vfill \pause

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\tikz[baseline=(t.base)]{
  \node[inner sep=0pt, outer sep=0pt, anchor=base] (t)
    {\emph{"use this test! it has been validated!"}}; \pause
  \draw[red, thick] ([yshift=0.35em]t.south west) -- ([yshift=0.35em]t.south east);
}
\end{center}
```


## More about validity
A test can have proofs of validity for different aspects

_E.g. a test may have evidence for validity to measure anxiety, and able to predict the success of outcome of a specific treatment_


## Validity: a continuum

Validity is not an all-or-none property
\vfill

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[baseline=(current bounding box.center)]


% : peggio - meglio
\node[anchor=west] at (0, 0) {\footnotesize \parbox[2cm]{4cm}{\emph{Less evidence on Validity}}};
\node[anchor=east] at (11, 0) {\footnotesize \parbox[2cm]{4cm}{\emph{More evidence on Validity}}};
\node[anchor=west] at (0, -1.2) {\textbf{Worse}};
\node[anchor=east] at (10, -1.2) {\textbf{Better}};
\draw[thick] (1.5, -1.2) -- (8.5, -1.2);

\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```

## Minimal value for Validity?
For some types of validity are associated to specific values, and there are recommended _minimal/desirable_ values. 

However: 

\pause
\vfill
- Sometimes tests are made available even if these requirement are not met. \pause
- Literature can differ on these reccomendation.


## Remember: Validity is a continuum

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[baseline=(current bounding box.center)]


% : peggio - meglio
\node[anchor=west] at (0, 0) {\footnotesize \parbox[2cm]{4cm}{\emph{Less evidence on Validity}}};
\node[anchor=east] at (11, 0) {\footnotesize \parbox[2cm]{4cm}{\emph{More evidence on Validity}}};
\node[anchor=west] at (0, -1.2) {\textbf{Worse}};
\node[anchor=east] at (10, -1.2) {\textbf{Better}};
\draw[thick] (1.5, -1.2) -- (8.5, -1.2);

\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
```



## Types of Validity

**A priori and a posteriori**

## A priori validity

Is the validity that can be assessed even before data collection has started and pertain the theoretical robustness of the instrument.
\pause
\vfill

- **Face Validity**
- **Content Validity**
 

## A posteriori validity

Is the validity as demonstrated after data are collected and actual properties of test items are assessed.
\pause
\vfill

- **Construct Validity** \scriptsize (Convergent/Discriminant and Factorial Structure) \normalsize
- **Criterion Validity** (Concurrent and Predictive)
- **Known-Groups Validity** (Concurrent and Predictive)
- **Ecological Validity**


## What should I remember?
\centering
![](Figures/Remembering_Validity.png){width="50%"}

\pause \small

1. That validity is an important property to check.

2. That you have resources (including this course) on relevant aspects about validity.
